+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [4 Press Releases]

+ BARTON MALOW + SHERIDAN CONSTRUCTION SET TO BUILD NEW MACON ARENA

MACON, GEORGIA - Barton Malow, in association with Sheridan Construction, has been chosen as the Construction Manager for the new Macon Arena, a major project that will replace the current Macon Coliseum and serve as a key part of the region's expanding entertainment opportunities.

Located southeast of Atlanta in Macon-Bibb County, the planned facility is designed to host various multipurpose events like minor league hockey, basketball games, high school tournaments, concerts, conferences, trade shows, and more. The new arena will offer a top-tier guest experience, featuring premium seating options like luxury suites, club suites, and loge boxes, as well as modern fan amenities such as clubs, concessions, restrooms, locker rooms, and integrated technologies. Back-of-house and support spaces will ensure the venue functions as a state-of-the-art civic facility, offering performers, event planners, and organizations a great experience.

"This arena won't be just a venue, it will be a destination and major economic engine that creates opportunities, attracts visitors, and keeps our city competitive for years to come," said Macon-Bibb Mayor Lester Miller.

As Macon's entertainment landscape continues to expand with the new Atrium Health Amphitheater and the recently renovated Macon City Auditorium, the new arena is poised to further elevate the region's growing lineup of elite venues.

"We're thrilled to partner with Sheridan Construction and bring Barton Malow's expertise to Macon-Bibb to deliver a top-tier arena. The new venue is set to become a central location in Georgia for sports, entertainment, and community gatherings," said Barton Malow Office Director Blake Boyd. "This project is an investment in Macon-Bibb County's future, and we're excited to support the local community every step of the way."

Adds Sheridan Construction President + CEO Christy Kovac. "It's a privilege for our firm to return to Macon's legacy of entertainment and civic pride, having built the original coliseum 57 years ago. Partnering with Barton Malow on this transformative new arena is both an honor and a responsibility. We believe this project will serve as a powerful economic and tourism engine, attracting visitors, creating opportunities, and enriching the lives of everyone in our community for generations to come."

Construction is planned to commence in summer 2026, with the project anticipated to reach completion in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY LUKE KNOCHEL PROMOTED TO VICE PRESIDENT OF PROJECT CONTROLS

Luke's project controls leadership role spans multiple geographic regions and consists of overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Barton Malow has announced the promotion of Luke Knochel to Vice President of Project Controls.

In his new role, Luke leads a multi‑disciplinary team responsible for core functions such as cost and schedule management, forecasting, project start‑up, accounts receivable and payable processes, and change management. His leadership spans multiple geographic regions, overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Luke started at Barton Malow in 2013 as a Cost Analyst and has served in various project controls and finance leadership roles throughout his tenure. He oversaw accounts payable and accounts receivable, change management, forecasting, reporting, and more on the Little Caesars Arena project, then went on to supervise Barton Malow's offices in Chicago, Columbus, and Nashville. After a promotion to Project Controls Manager overseeing the then-Midwest business unit, Luke transitioned into a corporate finance role managing the shared service department and implementing new accounts payable software.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY BUILDING FOR THE BRAND: CONSTRUCTING COLLEGE ATHLETIC FACILITIES IN THE NIL ERA

NIL athletic construction: Many modern facilities now include dedicated content creation zones where athletes can produce high-quality photos and videos for social media and sponsorship campaigns.

On July 1, 2021, the NCAA formally adopted a policy allowing college athletes to earn money for their name, image, and likeness (NIL).

At the time, one of my acquaintances, Matt Wilson, was serving as the Senior Director of Development for Athletics at Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University. He witnessed firsthand the immediate impact that NIL had on college athletics.

Today, Matt sees things from the other side of the table. After leaving Embry-Riddle in 2022, he spent two years as Executive Director of NIL Collective Operations + Development at Athlete Forward Initiative. Now he runs his own consulting group designed to provide leadership, fundraising, communication, and culture development training for college athletic departments.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW EARNS ECOVADIS BRONZE MEDAL FOR SUSTAINABILITY EXCELLENCE

Barton Malow has been awarded the EcoVadis Bronze Medal, placing the construction enterprise among the top 35% of companies worldwide for sustainability performance. This achievement underscores Barton Malow's leadership in green building practices, ethical business operations, and sustainable procurement.

"This recognition marks a significant milestone in our ongoing commitment to integrating sustainability principles across our business and reflects the progress we've made in partnership with our clients and stakeholders," said Senior Director of Sustainability Jill Katic.

EcoVadis, the world's most trusted provider of business sustainability ratings, evaluates companies on four key themes: Environment, Labor & Human Rights, Ethics, and Sustainable Procurement.

Barton Malow is committed to creating a positive impact on the planet, its people, and the communities in which it builds. To learn more about how Barton Malow is working towards its long-term environmental commitments, like reducing carbon emissions and implementing green building practices, explore the 2025 Sustainability Impact Report.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. DAVID CAMPBELL NAMED TO AZ BIG MEDIA'S 2026 "PEOPLE TO KNOW IN COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE" LIST

PHOENIX, Arizona (November 6, 2025) - PCL Buildings Inc. is pleased to announce that David Campbell, district manager for PCL Construction's Phoenix buildings office, has been named to AZ Big Media's "2026 People and Projects to Know in Commercial Real Estate" list. This annual recognition celebrates influential leaders who are shaping the future of Arizona's commercial real estate industry.

"This recognition highlights the momentum we're building in Arizona's construction market and our commitment to contributing positively to the region's future," says Campbell. "I'm proud to represent PCL and support the growth and innovation happening across Arizona."

Campbell was named as the leader of PCL's newest commercial buildings office in Phoenix this past spring, building upon a nearly 40-year legacy of excellence in water and civil construction across the Southwest. The Phoenix Buildings office marks a strategic milestone in PCL's commitment to the region, offering construction expertise across sectors including hospitality, manufacturing, aviation, and commercial and retail development.

His appearance on AZ Big Media's list reflects Campbell's contributions to the industry over the course of his nearly 20-year construction career, having led a wide range of high-profile projects across the U.S. Campbell joined PCL in 2005, with early contributions to entertainment and hospitality developments in Orlando. He later served as senior project manager on the American Dream megamall in New Jersey and held several leadership roles at PCL's U.S. head office in Denver before his current role in Phoenix.

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

"The gift of these teen suites is the culmination of our vision for this next chapter," said Tracy Leonard Warner, executive director of Ryan House. "This home has been serving families

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

urn:contentItem:6JGX-CJ33-RTFS-44TK-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:12ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 Press Releases]

+ SWINERTON TAPS PROVEN DUO TO DRIVE NATIONAL ACCOUNTS

With over $1 billion in growth and over 250 major projects under their belts, Swinerton's Chris Chany and Derek Jaschke step into new roles to drive client-focused expansion. Swinerton announces two strategic leadership promotions to continue delivering exceptional client experiences nationwide. Effective July 1, Chris Chany has been promoted to lead National Markets, while Derek Jaschke has been named Director of National Accounts, succeeding Chany.

Chany, known for his relationship-driven leadership style, previously served as Director of National Accounts, where he was instrumental in expanding Swinerton's client base and driving growth. Under his leadership, the national accounts sales model grew from $350 million to more than $1 billion in just four years. Chany has strengthened partnerships across multiple markets and sectors, generating repeat business.

"Chris' ability to strategize with teams from pre-sell to pursuit, through project execution and closeout, has allowed Swinerton to grow and solidify critical client relationships and seize growth opportunities for our teams," shares Don Adair, Swinerton's CRO. "Throughout his career, Chris has emphasized relationships, extraordinary client experiences, dependable project delivery and consistently generating repeat business."

Source: Company Website

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED WILLIAM SILVA NAMED DIRECTOR OF NATIONAL MASS TIMBER

Instrumental leader to enhance Swinerton Builders' mass timber capabilities Swinerton, a national commercial general contractor and construction manager with office locations nationwide, continues its commitment to advancing and accelerating the adoption of mass timber construction with the appointment of William Silva as Director, National Mass Timber.

In this position, Silva will lead the creation of a Mass Timber Center of Excellence, a cross-functional initiative designed by Swinerton to drive innovation, collaboration and integrate the company's extensive general contracting expertise with its affiliate firm, Timberlab. Timberlab's specialized capabilities include mass timber procurement, manufacturing, fabrication, engineering and design. The center will serve as a hub for innovation, education, and operational excellence, empowering Swinerton teams nationwide to deliver exceptional mass timber projects and continue to be a trusted resource for its clients, design partners, and engineering partners.

Source: Company Website

+ SWINERTON TOPS-OUT LATEST MASS TIMBER MULTIFAMILY PROJECT IN CHARLOTTE

Swinerton, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, announces the topping out of Cordo, an $80 million multifamily development in Charlotte's Optimist Park and the construction firm's fifth collaboration with Space Craft. Just blocks from The Joinery and Oxbow, also developed by Space Craft and built by Swinerton, Cordo is designed to be a walkable, transit-friendly community for active families and young professionals. The hybrid-structure broke ground last fall and is expected to be ready for occupancy by summer 2027.

"There is a distinct comfort level when you have a long-term relationship with your construction crew that spans multiple projects," says Brian Nicholson, director of construction, Space Craft. "Cordo is the fifth project we've embarked on with Swinerton, and the team continues to impress us with an unwavering commitment to quality and ability to deliver on our vision. With each community, we set out to build a sustainable, modern neighborhood for today. Swinerton has been instrumental in helping us shape the Mill District into one of Charlotte's hottest neighborhoods."

Situated on a three-acre site in Charlotte's Optimist Park community, Cordo totals seven floors with 3,000 square feet of street-level retail just steps from Cordelia Park, Optimist Hall and LYNX Blue Line light rail access. The 325,000-square foot development features 287studio, one-, two-, three- and four-bedroom units and offers premium amenities including a rooftop terrace, private courtyard, electric bicycles for rent, a fitness center, a shared dining area and coworking and meeting space. A Mass timber brings a biophilic element into each residence with cross-laminated timber (CLT) decks creating exposed timber ceilings. Charlotte architecture firm Shook Kelley designed the community located at 515 Parkwood Ave.

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [4 Press Releases]

+ BARTON MALOW + SHERIDAN CONSTRUCTION SET TO BUILD NEW MACON ARENA

MACON, GEORGIA - Barton Malow, in association with Sheridan Construction, has been chosen as the Construction Manager for the new Macon Arena, a major project that will replace the current Macon Coliseum and serve as a key part of the region's expanding entertainment opportunities.

Located southeast of Atlanta in Macon-Bibb County, the planned facility is designed to host various multipurpose events like minor league hockey, basketball games, high school tournaments, concerts, conferences, trade shows, and more. The new arena will offer a top-tier guest experience, featuring premium seating options like luxury suites, club suites, and loge boxes, as well as modern fan amenities such as clubs, concessions, restrooms, locker rooms, and integrated technologies. Back-of-house and support spaces will ensure the venue functions as a state-of-the-art civic facility, offering performers, event planners, and organizations a great experience.

"This arena won't be just a venue, it will be a destination and major economic engine that creates opportunities, attracts visitors, and keeps our city competitive for years to come," said Macon-Bibb Mayor Lester Miller.

As Macon's entertainment landscape continues to expand with the new Atrium Health Amphitheater and the recently renovated Macon City Auditorium, the new arena is poised to further elevate the region's growing lineup of elite venues.

"We're thrilled to partner with Sheridan Construction and bring Barton Malow's expertise to Macon-Bibb to deliver a top-tier arena. The new venue is set to become a central location in Georgia for sports, entertainment, and community gatherings," said Barton Malow Office Director Blake Boyd. "This project is an investment in Macon-Bibb County's future, and we're excited to support the local community every step of the way."

Adds Sheridan Construction President + CEO Christy Kovac. "It's a privilege for our firm to return to Macon's legacy of entertainment and civic pride, having built the original coliseum 57 years ago. Partnering with Barton Malow on this transformative new arena is both an honor and a responsibility. We believe this project will serve as a powerful economic and tourism engine, attracting visitors, creating opportunities, and enriching the lives of everyone in our community for generations to come."

Construction is planned to commence in summer 2026, with the project anticipated to reach completion in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY LUKE KNOCHEL PROMOTED TO VICE PRESIDENT OF PROJECT CONTROLS

Luke's project controls leadership role spans multiple geographic regions and consists of overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Barton Malow has announced the promotion of Luke Knochel to Vice President of Project Controls.

In his new role, Luke leads a multi‑disciplinary team responsible for core functions such as cost and schedule management, forecasting, project start‑up, accounts receivable and payable processes, and change management. His leadership spans multiple geographic regions, overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Luke started at Barton Malow in 2013 as a Cost Analyst and has served in various project controls and finance leadership roles throughout his tenure. He oversaw accounts payable and accounts receivable, change management, forecasting, reporting, and more on the Little Caesars Arena project, then went on to supervise Barton Malow's offices in Chicago, Columbus, and Nashville. + PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

PHOENIX, Arizona (November 6, 2025) - PCL Buildings Inc. is pleased to announce that David Campbell, district manager for PCL Construction's Phoenix buildings office, has been named to AZ Big Media's "2026 People and Projects to Know in Commercial Real Estate" list. This annual recognition celebrates influential leaders who are shaping the future of Arizona's commercial real estate industry.

"This recognition highlights the momentum we're building in Arizona's construction market and our commitment to contributing positively to the region's future," says Campbell. "I'm proud to represent PCL and support the growth and innovation happening across Arizona."

Campbell was named as the leader of PCL's newest commercial buildings office in Phoenix this past spring, building upon a nearly 40-year legacy of excellence in water and civil construction across the Southwest. The Phoenix Buildings office marks a strategic milestone in PCL's commitment to the region, offering construction expertise across sectors including hospitality, manufacturing, aviation, and commercial and retail development.

His appearance on AZ Big Media's list reflects Campbell's contributions to the industry over the course of his nearly 20-year construction career, having led a wide range of high-profile projects across the U.S. Campbell joined PCL in 2005, with early contributions to entertainment and hospitality developments in Orlando. + MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. + CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

urn:contentItem:6JGH-K913-SCG7-44KV-00000-00HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM KIEWIT CORPORATION AND PEERS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:17ZLexisNexisSTOCKHENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - RECENT NEWS UPDATE FROM KIEWIT CORPORATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO

PermID4296577863Websitehttp://www.henselphelps.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

Hensel Phelps Construction Co., Inc., founded in 1937, is an employee-owned general contractor headquartered in Greeley, Colorado. With over 4,000 employees across 10 U.S. offices, it delivers construction and renovation projects in aviation, commercial, healthcare, education, and government sectors. Generating over $6 billion annually, notable projects include the Pentagon renovation and San Jose International Airport modernization. In 2024, Hensel Phelps advanced its use of virtual design and construction (VDC) technologies, with VDC Manager Mark Blacklin winning a drone challenge for high-quality imaging. Its culture emphasizes safety, collaboration, and innovation, fostering a commitment to excellence through employee ownership. Competing with Turner Construction, Hensel Phelps stands out for its integrated project delivery and sustainable practices. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy projects and enhancing safety programs like Construction Safety Week.

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D SECURES MAJOR PARTNERSHIP WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR CONSTRUCTION TECHNOLOGY

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has marked a significant milestone by securing a multi-year agreement with Hensel Phelps, expanding its collaboration from a single pilot to the deployment of Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects. This announcement was made on 1st April 2026, emphasizing Track3D's growing influence in the construction sector.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize the use of Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, indicating strong confidence in the platform’s scalability and effectiveness across diverse construction projects. By utilizing automated progress tracking, Hensel Phelps aims to identify on-site issues earlier, which can significantly reduce delays and enhance schedule reliability, especially in complex builds.A notable case study from San Francisco International Airport highlighted the successful application of Track3D’s technology on a challenging infrastructure project. Project leaders reported a shift from reactive issue resolution to proactive decision-making, facilitated by 360deg site walks and the involvement of existing field personnel, thereby minimizing the need for major workflow changes. This deployment at SFO reinforces Track3D's relevance in high-value infrastructure work and solidifies its position in the reality intelligence market.In addition to the partnership with Hensel Phelps, Track3D has introduced a March update to its platform that focuses on large-enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. Key enhancements include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which are designed to provide major contractors with centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, the new capture-free progress tracking feature allows teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, promoting continuous oversight.Track3D is also intensifying its go-to-market strategy by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. The company aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and reduce manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Collectively, these developments—ranging from the Hensel Phelps portfolio rollout to enterprise-focused product enhancements—underscore Track3D’s strengthening competitive position in construction technology and may pave the way for sustainable growth in the future.

+ MICAH CHOY STEPS UP AS PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education’s Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a significant milestone in his career with the company, which he joined in 2018.During his tenure at Hensel Phelps, Choy has played a pivotal role in various major projects across the aviation, wastewater, and federal market sectors in Hawaii and Guam. His collaborative leadership style has garnered recognition, allowing him to build strong partnerships across teams and effectively drive project outcomes.Choy's promotion to project superintendent reflects Hensel Phelps' commitment to developing talent within the organization. His experience and expertise will be instrumental in managing the Regional Kitchen project, which is critical for enhancing educational facilities in Hawaii.The construction industry continues to evolve, and Hensel Phelps is positioning itself for future growth by promoting leaders like Choy. His ability to navigate complex projects while fostering teamwork aligns with the company's strategic goals.As Hensel Phelps embarks on new initiatives, Choy's leadership will likely contribute to the successful execution of high-profile projects. Stakeholders and investors will be watching closely as the company continues to build its reputation in the construction sector.

+ TRACK3D PARTNERS WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR MAJOR CONSTRUCTION ROLLOUT

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has announced a significant milestone in its growth trajectory as it secures a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps. As of 25th March 2026, this collaboration expands from a single pilot project to deployment across more than 200 active projects, marking a notable shift in how the construction industry embraces advanced technology.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform portfolio-wide for progress monitoring, demonstrating a strong confidence in the technology's scalability across diverse project types. This extensive rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue streams for Track3D, enhance customer loyalty, and establish a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In addition to the enterprise agreement, Track3D highlighted a successful case study at San Francisco International Airport. Hensel Phelps utilized Track3D's technology on this demanding build, where project leaders noted that the platform facilitated a shift from reactive to proactive decision-making. The deployment at SFO showcases how Track3D can enhance progress tracking and coordination across trades without necessitating significant changes to existing workflows.On the product development front, Track3D has announced a March release aimed at improving enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. New features include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which allow for centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, enhancements such as capture-free progress tracking capabilities enable teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, marking a significant improvement in operational flexibility for enterprise users.Track3D is also advancing its go-to-market strategy by focusing on specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. This event is pivotal for drywall and ceiling professionals, and Track3D aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and enhance margin protection. Overall, the recent developments with Hensel Phelps, the SFO case study, and the March product upgrades indicate a period of meaningful commercial traction for Track3D, potentially solidifying its competitive position in the construction technology landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PARTNERS IN $7.1 BILLION AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has secured a significant design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. This announcement was made on 28th April 2026, marking a pivotal moment in the development of public transit infrastructure in Austin, Texas.The planned railway system will encompass 15 stations along a 10-mile stretch, featuring all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes throughout most of the day. Kiewit Corporation, in collaboration with local partner Austin Commercial, will oversee the design and construction of the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched, ensuring operational efficiency.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide a workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, thereby contributing to the overall functionality of the light rail system. The selected team for this project includes 10 subcontractors focused on the design phase, which is anticipated to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.Greg Canally, CEO of the Austin Transit Partnership (ATP), emphasized the importance of this development, stating, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally highlighted that partnering with a world-class construction firm like Kiewit Corporation, which possesses deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success while fostering job creation and economic opportunities across Central Texas.Previously, an Aecom-led consortium named LINC Austin was awarded the role of delivery partner for the first phase of the larger rail project in December 2024. This consortium includes notable firms such as Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting, all of which will play a crucial role in the realization of Austin's light rail ambitions.

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION SELECTED FOR EAST AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A major piece of Austin's long-discussed light rail is finally moving from plans to prep work. On Wednesday, the Austin Transit Partnership signed off on Kiewit Austin Partnership as the design-build contractor for the light rail Operations and Maintenance Facility, clearing the way for pre-construction and inching a central Project Connect component closer to groundbreakers and bulldozers near the airport. The date of this significant development is 21st April 2026.According to Austin Transit Partnership, the ATP board approved Kiewit Austin Partnership—a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial—as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility. ATP CEO Greg Canally remarked, “This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system.” The agency's decision allows KAP to immediately launch pre-construction work, including advancing design, starting the permitting process, and preparing the site for future construction.The operations and maintenance facility is slated for a 62-acre site at the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, according to ATP. The planned footprint, near U.S. Highway 183 and State Highway 71, will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles and will necessitate property acquisitions affecting dozens of existing businesses. Local reporting has highlighted that roughly one-third of the businesses expected to be displaced are concentrated in Airport Commerce Park, raising concerns about relocation and neighborhood impacts.With KAP in place, ATP plans to expedite the Operations and Maintenance Facility design through permitting and site preparation, paving the way for the broader light rail project to advance toward full construction. Industry estimates indicate that mainline construction could commence in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration's combined Final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision, published in January 2026, has cleared the project to enter final design and begin land acquisition.ATP noted that the KAP team already includes around 10 subcontractors during the design phase, with opportunities expected to increase once construction ramps up. The larger Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. On the operations side, the maintenance facility is expected to support approximately 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, further contributing to local economic growth.

+ ATERNIUM ENGAGES KIEWIT FOR HYDROGEN PRODUCTION FACILITY PRE-FEED

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its inaugural integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility. This strategic move comes as Aternium aims to advance its ambitious $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026, signaling a significant step in the company's broader, multi-plant development strategy focused on establishing a robust infrastructure for advanced energy materials.The planned facility will be located in the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States, designed to meet the growing demand for low-carbon energy solutions. Aternium's initiative not only seeks to enhance domestic capabilities in the production of heavy water—currently sourced primarily from countries like China and Iran—but also aims to address critical supply chain vulnerabilities. Andrew Cottone, Founder and CEO of Aternium, emphasized the importance of this collaboration with Kiewit, citing shared values in technical expertise and safety standards.According to Cottone, the initiative is poised to create significant socioeconomic benefits by fostering workforce development and job creation in local communities. Aternium's focus on integrating advanced technologies and innovative processes is expected to stimulate regional economic growth, thereby supporting the transition to cleaner energy systems. Kiewit will play a pivotal role in executing Aternium's unique business model, which aims to deliver both clean hydrogen and heavy water at competitive costs.Kiewit’s involvement in the project brings extensive experience in engineering, procurement, and construction within the energy sector. Tyler Nordquist, Executive Vice President at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., expressed confidence in the partnership, highlighting the company's ability to add substantial value during the pre-FEED phase. This phase will include defining the project's scope, optimizing design parameters, and laying a solid foundation for future development.Aternium's integrated production approach is noteworthy as it combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water. This dual-output model not only aims to reduce hydrogen production costs but also addresses a critical vulnerability in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. With the increasing demand for clean energy and high-tech components, Aternium's initiative is strategically positioned at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing, thus offering a scalable solution that aligns with both economic and technological priorities.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION MARKS KEY MILESTONE WITH ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER

FREDERICK, MD — Turner Construction Company celebrated the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, marking the completion of the structural phase for the 72-megawatt facility. This significant milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after breaking ground, showcasing the effectiveness of early planning and strong collaboration among the project team.To date, the project has seen over 80,000 craft hours logged, with approximately 345 skilled workers contributing to its progress. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," stated Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently."The Aligned IAD-06 Data Center spans 450,000 square feet and is situated on a 75-acre campus designed to meet the increasing digital infrastructure demands of the region. The facility features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines both liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize operational performance.The successful topping out of the IAD-06 Data Center underscores Turner Construction's expertise in managing complex projects in the data center sector, which is rapidly evolving to accommodate the surge in digital services. The collaborative efforts demonstrated by Turner and its partners are pivotal in ensuring that the project meets its ambitious timeline and quality standards.As the project moves into the next phases of development, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the advancements made by Turner Construction and Aligned. With the structural phase completed, the focus will shift to the interior fit-out and operational readiness of the facility, further solidifying its role in enhancing the region's digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMMENCES VERTICAL BUILD ON $900M GEISINGER EXPANSION

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has begun vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center, marking a major milestone in one of northeastern Pennsylvania’s largest health care investments. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.After years of planning and foundational work, steel is now rising on the 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower. This new structure will increase the hospital's capacity from 356 to 500 beds and expand the number of operating rooms from 12 to 18. Additionally, the emergency department will be enlarged, supporting Geisinger’s goal of providing all private patient rooms. The facility is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028.David Kaminski, Vice President & Senior General Manager at Turner Construction, stated, “This milestone marks an important step forward in delivering expanded, modern health care access for families across northeastern Pennsylvania. We are proud to partner with Geisinger on a transformational project that will enhance patient care, support caregivers, and strengthen this community for decades to come.”The project is anticipated to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact. The onsite workforce is expected to grow from 150 to as many as 450 workers during peak activity. Upon completion, Geisinger plans to hire an additional 400 employees, further contributing to local job creation.Originally opened in 1981, the Geisinger Wyoming Valley campus has expanded over the decades to meet the growing demand for health services. This latest addition will bring advanced medical capabilities comparable to those found in major academic medical centers located in large metropolitan areas.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR AMBITIOUS WESTCOURT ORLANDO PROJECT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, an ambitious development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone in the project’s progression.The decision to partner with Turner Construction stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures, the project developer. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, noted that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, especially along Florida’s Space Coast, which includes coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches. Chapman recalled an early meeting where he expressed confidence in their ability to tackle the Westcourt project, saying, “If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us.”Turner Construction has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, focusing on pre-construction activities alongside JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Jeff Justen, vice president and general manager of Turner’s Orlando Business Unit, indicated that the early site activities have commenced, including utility mapping and surveying. These preparatory measures are critical for ensuring a smooth transition into the next phases of construction.The Westcourt project has faced some delays, shifting timelines due to financing challenges and fluctuations in construction costs. However, Chapman emphasized that involving Turner Construction early in the process allowed for valuable input on design and execution strategies. “We don’t want to design in a vacuum,” he said, highlighting the importance of contractor collaboration during the planning stages.Valued at approximately $500 million, the Westcourt development aims to create a vibrant mixed-use space featuring a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center. During peak construction, the project is expected to support around 800 workers on-site, contributing to the local job market in Central Florida. Chapman expressed excitement over the project’s complexity, stating that JMA Ventures is drawn to intricate developments, which informed their choice in partners like Turner Construction.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF $3.7B CALIFORNIA TOWER

McCarthy Holdings has reached a significant construction milestone with the topping out of the $3.7 billion California Tower on February 6, 2026. This achievement comes a year and a half after breaking ground on the ambitious project in 2024, as reported by UC Davis Health. The California Tower is designed using a progressive design-build delivery method, marking a pivotal moment in the expansion of UC Davis' Sacramento campus.The California Tower is part of an extensive eight-year plan to double the size of the UC Davis campus, which is recognized as the largest health system capital expansion currently underway in the United States. Once completed, the 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion will house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities dedicated to pharmacy and burn care units. Notably, the tower will also introduce approximately 334 additional inpatient beds to enhance patient care capabilities.In a customary celebration, stakeholders in the project gathered to sign the beam, which was ceremonially placed to mark this important milestone. UC Davis Health anticipates the opening of the California Tower in 2030. This state-of-the-art building is the final component of UC Davis Health's Vision 2030 plan, which aims to increase the campus's total building space from 3.6 million square feet to over 7 million square feet.The California Tower is not McCarthy Holdings' only recent achievement in the healthcare sector. The company completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December. While healthcare construction has shown resilience, contributing to overall growth in construction planning at the end of 2025, early indicators from Dodge's first report of 2026 suggest a slowdown in healthcare project planning at the start of the year.As McCarthy Holdings continues to make strides in the healthcare construction sector, the successful topping out of the California Tower signifies an important step in fulfilling the growing demand for healthcare infrastructure. Investors and stakeholders will be closely watching how these developments unfold as the company forges ahead with its ambitious projects.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS ADVANCES SUSTAINABILITY WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction firms in the U.S., has released its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Report. The report was published on 16th February 2026, and reflects McCarthy's commitment to strengthening its sustainability efforts across its operations and subsidiaries.Developed in partnership with Gravity, a leading enterprise carbon accounting and energy management platform, the report provides a comprehensive view of McCarthy's carbon footprint. It covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and key categories of Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 having undergone independent auditing and awarded limited assurance. This commitment to transparency and accountability aligns with McCarthy's mission to be the best builder in America.Ian Pope, Senior Director of Sustainability at McCarthy Holdings, emphasized the company's dedication to community building. 'As an employee-owned company, this important effort is in line with our core values and provides our teams with the practical data necessary to meet regulatory requirements,' he stated. McCarthy's sustainability strategy is anchored in three primary priorities: understanding its environmental impact, acting responsibly for its people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption.In addition to emissions reporting, McCarthy Holdings integrates responsible practices into its projects. This includes biodiversity initiatives, responsible fuel management, and the use of digital tools and artificial intelligence for smarter project decisions. The company is also focusing on responsible end-of-life management for solar panels through its advanced PV recycling program, partnering with Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and support a circular economy.By leveraging emissions data and operational insights, McCarthy Holdings continues to demonstrate that its role as a builder of communities extends to fostering a more sustainable future. With a commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being, McCarthy is setting benchmarks in the construction industry for responsible practices.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES JOINS CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP AS HR DIRECTOR

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 Press Releases]

+ SWINERTON TAPS PROVEN DUO TO DRIVE NATIONAL ACCOUNTS

With over $1 billion in growth and over 250 major projects under their belts, Swinerton's Chris Chany and Derek Jaschke step into new roles to drive client-focused expansion. Swinerton announces two strategic leadership promotions to continue delivering exceptional client experiences nationwide. Effective July 1, Chris Chany has been promoted to lead National Markets, while Derek Jaschke has been named Director of National Accounts, succeeding Chany.

Chany, known for his relationship-driven leadership style, previously served as Director of National Accounts, where he was instrumental in expanding Swinerton's client base and driving growth. Under his leadership, the national accounts sales model grew from $350 million to more than $1 billion in just four years. Chany has strengthened partnerships across multiple markets and sectors, generating repeat business.

"Chris' ability to strategize with teams from pre-sell to pursuit, through project execution and closeout, has allowed Swinerton to grow and solidify critical client relationships and seize growth opportunities for our teams," shares Don Adair, Swinerton's CRO. "Throughout his career, Chris has emphasized relationships, extraordinary client experiences, dependable project delivery and consistently generating repeat business."

Source: Company Website

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED WILLIAM SILVA NAMED DIRECTOR OF NATIONAL MASS TIMBER

Instrumental leader to enhance Swinerton Builders' mass timber capabilities Swinerton, a national commercial general contractor and construction manager with office locations nationwide, continues its commitment to advancing and accelerating the adoption of mass timber construction with the appointment of William Silva as Director, National Mass Timber.

In this position, Silva will lead the creation of a Mass Timber Center of Excellence, a cross-functional initiative designed by Swinerton to drive innovation, collaboration and integrate the company's extensive general contracting expertise with its affiliate firm, Timberlab. Timberlab's specialized capabilities include mass timber procurement, manufacturing, fabrication, engineering and design. The center will serve as a hub for innovation, education, and operational excellence, empowering Swinerton teams nationwide to deliver exceptional mass timber projects and continue to be a trusted resource for its clients, design partners, and engineering partners.

Source: Company Website

+ SWINERTON TOPS-OUT LATEST MASS TIMBER MULTIFAMILY PROJECT IN CHARLOTTE

Swinerton, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, announces the topping out of Cordo, an $80 million multifamily development in Charlotte's Optimist Park and the construction firm's fifth collaboration with Space Craft. Just blocks from The Joinery and Oxbow, also developed by Space Craft and built by Swinerton, Cordo is designed to be a walkable, transit-friendly community for active families and young professionals. The hybrid-structure broke ground last fall and is expected to be ready for occupancy by summer 2027.

"There is a distinct comfort level when you have a long-term relationship with your construction crew that spans multiple projects," says Brian Nicholson, director of construction, Space Craft. "Cordo is the fifth project we've embarked on with Swinerton, and the team continues to impress us with an unwavering commitment to quality and ability to deliver on our vision. With each community, we set out to build a sustainable, modern neighborhood for today. Swinerton has been instrumental in helping us shape the Mill District into one of Charlotte's hottest neighborhoods."

Situated on a three-acre site in Charlotte's Optimist Park community, Cordo totals seven floors with 3,000 square feet of street-level retail just steps from Cordelia Park, Optimist Hall and LYNX Blue Line light rail access. The 325,000-square foot development features 287studio, one-, two-, three- and four-bedroom units and offers premium amenities including a rooftop terrace, private courtyard, electric bicycles for rent, a fitness center, a shared dining area and coworking and meeting space. A Mass timber brings a biophilic element into each residence with cross-laminated timber (CLT) decks creating exposed timber ceilings. Charlotte architecture firm Shook Kelley designed the community located at 515 Parkwood Ave.

Source: Company Website

DALLAS - Austin is honored to once again be named the No. 1 Science + Technology Facility Construction Firm in the U.S. by World of Construction.

From semiconductor fabs to digital infrastructure, our teams pour their skill, care, and dedication into creating the specialized spaces that help fuel tomorrow's breakthroughs.
